title: Full English breakfast
subtitle: Bacon, sausage, eggs, beans, toast — sequenced.
excerpt: The full plate: bacon, sausages, fried eggs, grilled tomato, mushrooms, baked beans, black pudding, and buttered toast, all hitting the table hot. The trick is sequencing — what goes on first, what goes on last, and where every pan sits while you build it. Serves two.
sourceNotes: The Edwardian English breakfast tradition is documented in Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861, Project Gutenberg #10136), as a sequence of separate cooked dishes brought to the table together. The contemporary 'all-on-one-plate' shape settled mid-twentieth century in transport cafés and seaside boarding houses.

recipe.freezeNotes: Nothing on the plate keeps well frozen; it's a from-scratch breakfast.
recipe.batchNotes: Two is the comfortable maximum at a domestic hob; the eggs in particular drop in quality cooked in waves.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Lay out everything before you start. The plate comes together fast once the heat is on.
recipe.temperatureNote: oven hot for grill finish; hob mostly medium

recipeTools.notes: Big enough for bacon and sausages together.
For tomatoes and mushrooms under the grill.

content.text: Two rashers of back bacon, two pork sausages, two eggs, two halved tomatoes, a handful of chestnut mushrooms, a slice of black pudding, a pool of baked beans, two slices of buttered toast, all hitting the plate hot enough to butter the bread on. Twenty-five minutes from cold hob to first bite, if the sequence is right.

Sequencing is everything. Sausages take the longest, so they start first. Mushrooms and tomatoes go under the grill while the sausages brown. Bacon and black pudding go in the second half. Beans warm in their own pan. Eggs are last; they take two minutes. The toast pops at the last second.
Heat {{rapeseed-oil}} of rapeseed oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add {{sausages-pork}} of pork sausages. Cook for 15 minutes, turning every three minutes, until evenly bronzed all over.

Heat the grill to high. Spread {{mushrooms-chestnut}} of mushrooms and {{tomato}} of halved tomatoes on a baking tray. Drizzle with a teaspoon of oil; season. Grill 8 minutes until the mushrooms release their water and the tomatoes blister.

Push the sausages to one side of the frying pan. Lay {{back-bacon}} of bacon rashers and {{black-pudding}} of black pudding slices in the gap. Cook for 3 minutes a side. The bacon fat crisps, the pudding's surface darkens.
Warm the beans, toast the bread
Tip {{baked-beans}} of baked beans into a small saucepan over a low hob; warm gently, stirring now and then. Drop {{bread}} slices of bread into the toaster.

Heat a teaspoon of oil in a small frying pan over medium heat. Crack {{eggs}} eggs into the pan. Cook for 2 minutes; spoon hot oil over the whites to set the tops without flipping. The yolks stay runny.

Butter the hot toast with {{salted-butter}} of salted butter. Divide everything between two warm plates: sausages, bacon, black pudding, tomatoes, mushrooms, a pool of beans, the egg crowning the lot. Toast on the side. Eat now; the plate cools fast.

items.symptom: Half the plate cold by the time the rest is ready
items.fix: Start sausages first. Bacon, mushrooms, tomatoes second wave. Eggs and toast last 2 minutes.
Medium heat; spoon hot oil over to set the whites. Pull at 2 minutes.

items.cause: Too thin, or cooked too high.
items.fix: Buy thick-cut back bacon; cook medium.

items.cause: Grill too close, or tomatoes too thick.
items.fix: Halve across the equator, not pole to pole. Eight minutes under the grill is the minimum.
Spread in a single layer. The water has to escape.

content.text: The full English is the British weekend breakfast — and the British transport-café midweek lunch, and the British seaside hotel-room-service order at 11 a.m. Each region claims its own variation: the full Scottish adds tattie scones and haggis; the Ulster fry brings soda farls; the full Welsh swaps in laverbread; the West Country slips in hog's pudding. The plate above is the southern English baseline. A pot of strong tea is the only correct drink.
